titel: Requirements for municipal land survey authorities' case management systems
html:
The proposal suggests changes to the law (1995:1393) concerning municipal land surveying authorities, introducing a requirement that these authorities use the case management system and other technical support tools provided by the national land surveying authority.

This change is proposed to take effect on January 1, 2027.

Proposed resolution of the Parliament

Prop. 2025/26:257

Government Proposal: The Riksdag (Parliament) shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (1995:1393) concerning municipal land surveying authorities. 3

Prop. 2025/26:257 2 Proposed amendment to the Act (1995:1393) concerning municipal land surveying authorities.

Hereby, with regard to the Act (1995:1393) concerning municipal land surveying authorities, it is stipulated that sections 2 and 8 shall have the following wording, and that two new sections, 5a and 5b, shall be introduced with the following wording.

Current wording | Proposed wording ---|--- 2 § | 2 § For a permit to be granted, the municipality must demonstrate that: | For a permit to be granted, the municipality must demonstrate that:

  1. the authority will have the entire municipality as its operational area. | 1. the authority will have the entire municipality as its operational area.
  2. the authority's tasks will substantially be limited to surveying activities and authority services related to surveying activities. | 2. the authority's tasks will substantially be limited to surveying activities and authority services related to surveying activities.
  3. the authority will have an organization suitable for the purpose and an operation that can be calculated to have | 3. the authority will have an organization suitable for the purpose and an operation that can be calculated to have

sufficient scope to maintain the necessary expertise. | sufficient scope to maintain the necessary expertise.

  1. at least two officials within the authority will have the necessary expertise and experience to handle surveying tasks. | 4. at least two officials within the authority will have the necessary expertise and experience to handle surveying tasks.
  2. the authority's information system will have a technical standard that ensures the exchange of information between the state and the municipality, which is necessary in the field of land surveying. | 5. the authority's information system will have a technical standard that ensures the exchange of information between the state and the municipality, which is necessary in the field of land surveying.

5a § A municipal land surveying authority shall use the case management systems and other technical support tools provided by the state land surveying authority.

5b § The government or the authority that the government designates may issue regulations regarding the provision and use of the technical support tools referred to in section 5a and regarding fees for their use.

8 § 1 If the activities of the municipal land surveying authority do not meet the conditions specified in section 2 or if the authority, in any significant respect, lacks the necessary authority functions, the municipality's permit may be revoked by the government after notification from the state land surveying authority. | If the activities of the municipal land surveying authority do not meet the conditions specified in sections 2 and 5a or the regulations issued pursuant to section 5b, or if the authority, in any significant respect, lacks the necessary authority functions, the municipality's permit may be revoked by the government after notification from the state land surveying authority.

This law shall enter into force on January 1, 2027. 1 Last amended 2008:532. 5

Prop. 2025/26:257 3
tldr: Municipalities that handle land surveying matters will be required to use the same digital system as the national authority, Lantmäteriet, starting in 2027. This is being implemented to ensure that the technology functions seamlessly between municipalities and the national government.
lattlast:
The government has proposed changes to the regulations governing municipal land surveying authorities. A land surveying authority is the part of a municipality that handles matters related to land and boundaries. Starting January 1, 2027, these municipal authorities will be required to use the same digital systems to manage their cases as the national authority, Lantmäteriet, uses.

The purpose of this change is to ensure that the technical tools and support for processing are the same. This is about making it possible to share information easily and securely between municipalities and the state. If a municipality does not use the correct systems, the government may revoke its permission to operate a land surveying service.

The government, or an authority designated by the government, may also determine the rules for how these technical systems should be used. They may also determine whether there will be costs associated with using the systems through various fees.

The proposal means that the requirements for what a municipality must demonstrate in order to start its own land surveying authority will be clearer. This includes having the right personnel, the right organization, and ensuring that the technology functions for the exchange of information between the state and the municipality.
